# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book exercises every capability declared in `_extension.yml`, so `quarto render` validates the extension in one shot.
It is the canonical demo for [`hebstr-book`](https://github.com/hebstr/quarto-hebstr-book), the multi-chapter equivalent of `example.qmd` in `hebstr-doc`.

The chapters cover:

1. **Typography & Text** — fonts, headings, lists, inline styles, custom CSS classes, block quote, inline math.
2. **Code & Data** — figures, tables, code blocks, code windows, script injection, tabsets.
3. **Callouts & References** — all callout types, display equations, cross-references, citations.

The body font is **Luciole** (sans-serif, designed for low-vision readers).
Inline `code()` uses **Fira Code** with programming ligatures.
Light and dark themes are available via the toggle in the sidebar.
